homepage.heroSubline: We fix cars right the first time. No upsells, just reliable service for Memphis drivers.
aboutText: Onyx Auto Repair started when founder Marcus Johnson saw too many Memphis drivers getting overcharged for basic repairs. What began as a two-bay shop on Summer Avenue has grown into a full-service facility with 20 technicians.
We believe in diagnosing problems accurately, explaining options clearly, and never recommending unnecessary work. Our technicians stay current with training on all major makes and models.
When we're not under the hood, you'll find us sponsoring youth programs at the Memphis Athletic League and supporting local food drives. This city's been good to us - we aim to return the favor.

valueProps.title: Same-day diagnostics
valueProps.description: Most vehicles get a full diagnosis within 2 hours so you know exactly what's needed.

valueProps.title: Memphis-tested parts
valueProps.description: We use components proven to withstand our pothole-filled roads and humid summers.

valueProps.title: Loaner cars available
valueProps.description: Don't miss work while we repair your vehicle - ask about our courtesy loaner program.

valueProps.title: Upfront pricing
valueProps.description: You'll approve every repair with a written estimate before we turn a wrench.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call or book online for same-week appointments at our Memphis shop.

services.name: Brake Repair
services.shortDescription: Complete brake inspections and repairs for Memphis drivers
services.description:
Memphis' stop-and-go traffic on I-240 and downtown streets wears brake components faster than most cities. We perform thorough brake inspections measuring pad thickness, rotor condition, and fluid quality.

Common repairs include replacing warped rotors (common after Tennessee's rainy seasons), addressing stuck calipers from road salt residue, and fixing ABS sensor issues. We only install ceramic or semi-metallic pads that handle our summer heat without fading.

Unlike quick-lube shops, we measure brake fluid boiling points and flush contaminated systems. All brake work includes a 12-mile test drive through Memphis' hilly neighborhoods to verify proper performance.
process: Step 1: Road test to assess pedal feel and noise
Step 2: Measure all components with digital calipers
Step 3: Present repair options with price comparisons
Step 4: Post-repair test drive through downtown traffic
benefits: Quieter stops on Memphis' brick-paved streets
Faster response in sudden Midtown traffic
Longer component life in humid conditions
ABS systems that work when roads ice over

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers replace brake fluid?
faq.answer: We recommend every 30,000 miles. Humidity causes brake fluid to absorb moisture faster here, lowering its boiling point.

faq.question: Why do my brakes squeak after crossing the Mississippi River?
faq.answer: The bridge's metal grating can temporarily deposit microscopic debris on rotors. We can resurface them if noise persists.

services.name: Engine Diagnostics
services.shortDescription: Advanced troubleshooting for check engine lights
services.description:
Modern vehicles average 30+ computer modules that communicate differently in Memphis' temperature swings. Our Snap-On scan tools read manufacturer-specific codes that generic code readers miss.

We start by replicating your concern - whether it's rough idling at Poplar Avenue stoplights or hesitation climbing the Hernando DeSoto Bridge. Live data monitoring helps identify intermittent issues that disappear when the car reaches the shop.

Common Memphis-specific fixes include cleaning throttle bodies gummed up from short trips, replacing oxygen sensors corroded by road salt, and reprogramming modules affected by voltage spikes from our aging power grid. We provide printed diagnostic reports you can share with your insurance company.
process: Step 1: Code retrieval with OEM-level scanners
Step 2: Live data recording during test drive
Step 3: Voltage and resistance testing at connectors
Step 4: Verification repair solved the root cause
benefits: Maintains fuel efficiency on long drives

faq.question: Why does my check engine light come on after heavy rain?
faq.answer: Water can temporarily affect sensor connections. If it stays on, we check for leaks in the air intake system.

faq.question: How quickly should I address an emissions-related code?
faq.answer: Within 2 weeks to pass Shelby County emissions testing. We provide free retests after repairs.

services.name: AC Repair
services.shortDescription: Cooling system repairs for Memphis summers
services.description:
With heat indexes regularly exceeding 100°F, a working AC system isn't optional in Memphis. We pressure test systems to find leaks that worsen in our humidity, often at Schrader valves or condenser fins damaged by highway debris.

Our EPA-certified technicians recover refrigerant properly rather than venting it illegally. We flush contaminated systems that develop sludge from infrequent use during mild winters. For older vehicles, we offer upgrades to more efficient R134a systems.

Every AC service includes cleaning condenser coils clogged with cottonwood pollen and testing compressor clutch operation. We measure vent temperatures to ensure your system cools properly even when idling in Graceland traffic.
process: Step 1: Performance test measuring vent temperatures
Step 2: Leak detection with UV dye or nitrogen
Step 3: Evacuation and recharge by weight
Step 4: Airflow measurement at all vents
benefits: Faster cooldowns after parking in the sun

faq.question: Why does my AC work poorly at red lights?
faq.answer: Likely a failing cooling fan. We test fan operation separately from the AC system.

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers recharge their AC?
faq.answer: Never - a properly sealed system maintains charge for years. Needing annual recharging indicates a leak.

services.name: Transmission Service
services.shortDescription: Fluid services and repairs for automatic transmissions
services.description:
Memphis drivers average more gear changes per mile than most cities due to our short blocks and frequent traffic lights. We perform transmission services tailored to your driving patterns - whether you're commuting on I-40 daily or making deliveries downtown.

Our fluid exchanges use manufacturer-approved fluids rather than generic substitutes. For vehicles frequently crossing the Mississippi River bridges, we check for water contamination that accelerates wear. Harsh shifts common in stop-and-go traffic often indicate solenoid issues we can repair without full rebuilds.

Every service includes scanning for stored codes, checking fluid condition with a spectrometer, and test driving through our hilly neighborhoods to verify smooth operation. We'll advise if your transmission shows signs needing more extensive work.
process: Step 1: Scan for shift adaptation codes
Step 2: Fluid analysis for metal particles
Step 3: Pan removal to inspect magnets
Step 4: Road test through varying grades

faq.question: Why does my transmission shift hard when it's cold?
faq.answer: Thickened fluid is common below 40°F. We check if symptoms persist after warmup.

faq.question: How often should I service my CVT transmission?
faq.answer: Every 60,000 miles for most models. The humid climate breaks down CVT fluid faster here.

services.name: Suspension Repair
services.description:
Memphis roads rank among Tennessee's worst for potholes and uneven pavement. We perform suspension inspections measuring play in ball joints, control arm bushings, and tie rod ends - all common failure points from our rough streets.

Our alignment specialists use Hunter's latest digital systems to account for Memphis' crowned roads and brick-paved surfaces. We recommend firmer shock absorbers for drivers regularly navigating I-240's expansion joints. For trucks hauling loads across the river, we check leaf spring condition and U-bolt torque.

Every suspension repair includes measuring ride height before and after work, plus a test drive down known rough roads like Lamar Avenue to verify improvements. We'll show you worn parts so you understand exactly what needed replacement.
process: Step 1: Bounce test measuring damping
Step 2: Measurement of all suspension angles
Step 3: Road force measurement on tires
Step 4: Post-repair alignment verification

faq.question: Why does my car pull after crossing train tracks?
faq.answer: Impact may have knocked alignment out. We check for bent components after such events.

faq.question: How often should Memphis drivers replace shocks?
faq.answer: Every 50,000 miles typically. Heavy urban wear accelerates need for replacement.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: The team at Onyx saved me from replacing perfectly good rotors when another shop said I needed full brake service. They showed me the measurements and only replaced what was necessary. My Expedition stops smoothly now even coming down the I-55 bridge.

reviews.name: Latoya M.
reviews.text: After my AC quit during last July's heat wave, Onyx had me back on the road same day. Found a leaking condenser that three other shops missed. Their loaner car kept my kids cool while mine was being fixed. Fair price and no pressure to upsell.

reviews.name: Robert K.
reviews.text: My Silverado was slipping gears pulling trailers across the river. Onyx diagnosed a bad solenoid pack instead of pushing for a full rebuild like others suggested. Six months later it's still shifting perfectly, even hauling lumber to job sites daily.

reviews.name: Denise W.
reviews.text: The check engine light in my Prius kept coming on after heavy rain. Onyx traced it to a cracked wiring harness rubbing against the firewall - something the dealership couldn't find in three visits. Detailed explanation and fair labor rate.

reviews.name: Marcus B.
reviews.text: Fixed my Cadillac's wandering steering after hitting a pothole on Union Ave. They aligned it perfectly and showed me the bent tie rod. Car drives straighter now than when I bought it. Honest mechanics are hard to find in Memphis.

reviews.name: Sarah J.
reviews.text: I appreciate how Onyx explained why Memphis humidity causes brake fluid to degrade faster. Full flush made my pedal feel firmer immediately. They even adjusted my parking brake for free while it was in for service. Will bring all our family cars here.

serviceAreas.content:
Onyx Auto Repair proudly serves the Memphis community with top-tier auto repair services. Whether you need routine maintenance, brake repairs, or engine diagnostics, our skilled technicians are here to keep your vehicle running smoothly. We understand the importance of reliable transportation in a bustling city like Memphis, and we're committed to providing fast, affordable, and honest service.

Located in the heart of Shelby County, our shop is easily accessible to all Memphis residents. We specialize in both domestic and foreign vehicles, offering everything from oil changes to complex electrical repairs. Our team stays up-to-date with the latest automotive technologies to ensure your car receives the best care possible.
serviceAreas.localContext: Memphis is known for its vibrant music scene, Mississippi River access, and being home to FedEx's global hub.
